jobs.description:
In this Individual Contributor role, you will own a portfolio of Hub Delivery Partners across delivery stations in your assigned region. You will develop and execute territory plans that drive partner launch success, sustained high performance, and retention while collaborating cross functionally with Station Operations Managers and Program Managers to solve complex problems in Amazon's evolving last mile delivery network.

Key job responsibilities
Partner Performance & Development

* Conduct performance reviews analyzing partner health and reliability metrics to provide data-driven recommendations
* Execute coaching interventions on operational best practices to achieve and maintain performance benchmarks
* Monitor performance trends and implement proactive interventions to prevent deactivation
* Serve as primary escalation point for partner issues, coordinating rapid resolution across internal teams
* Communicate operational impacts, program updates, and network changes with urgency and clarity to external stakeholders.

Strategic Partnership

* Act as partner advocate, channeling feedback to drive systemic improvements across Hub Delivery operations
* Identify workflow inefficiencies and formulate network-level operational improvements
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality solutions addressing partner pain points

Capacity & Growth Management

* Partner with planning teams on volume forecasting and capacity optimization
* Support partner scaling initiatives while maintaining quality standards
* Analyze territory performance data to identify expansion opportunities and risk mitigation strategies

This high-impact role requires strong communication skills, analytical problem-solving ability, and ownership mentality in ambiguous environments. You will build processes from scratch while driving innovation in Amazon's last mile delivery space.


About the team

jobs.description:
We are seeking to hire research engineers for robot dexterous manipulation with the goal of developing dexterous manipulation systems at human-level performance. Our strategy focuses on a real2sim2real pipeline that learns from large scale human videos and leverage simulation and reinforcement learning to bridge the human-to-robot embodiment gap.

Key job responsibilities
• Learning dexterous manipulation: Design and implement robot learning algorithms for manipulation
• Leverage vision models to extract 3D hand-object manipulation information from real-world situations
• Develop large-scale real2sim2real pipeline for dexterous manipulation
• Multimodal Sensor Fusion: Integrate tactile sensing, proprioception and vision into the learning pipeline

jobs.description:
The Buyer Risk Prevention (BRP) team is looking for a Finance Manager to manage Finance Planning and Analysis (FP&A), including budgeting, forecasting, and headcount management for BRP. BRP is part of the Selling Partner Services, which organization employees over 100,000+ employees who provide support to Amazon's Selling Partners while also ensuring that we protect Amazon customers from bad actors. In this role you will support headcount and expense forecasting and reporting for the organization. Additionally, you will help support the Buyer Abuse Prevention team who works to prevent abuse of our concessions policies and take actions to protect customer accounts. This role actively helps the business identify areas to reduce costs while maintaining a high quality of service for Amazon's Customers.



Key job responsibilities
• Drive standardization and automation of planning and reporting processes, adhering to deadlines
• Analyze HC and Opex variances vs. plan and provide insight to business partners including cost-saving and process improvement recommendations
• Partner with the business to make trade-off decisions regarding headcount resourcing and support levels
• Lead ad-hoc cost / benefit analyses, financial modeling and business case development to support new initiatives and business decisions
• Support internal customer groups in all finance matters including budgeting, forecasting and financial controls

jobs.description:
The Americas Deliver Service Partner (DSP) team partners with entrepreneurial individuals across the globe to build sustainable and scalable delivery capacity so that Amazon can continue to delight customers with fast, efficient, and bar raising fulfillment of their orders. Equal parts advisor and operating partner, Amazon’s team of Business Coaches assist our DSP business owners as they build and grow successful last mile package delivery businesses. As trusted advisors, Business Coaches work alongside DSPs to help solve their biggest challenges at a programmatic level; influence their business planning and strategy; and look around corners to identify obstacles and recommend solutions, ensuring DSP owners make effective business decisions and are setup for success.
The DSP Shared Services team (DSS) is the backbone of the Americas DSP organization and it is their workstreams that enable the scale of the program. This Account Manager, DSP Shared Services will be responsible for providing support on a day to day processes that enable DSPs to continue to grow and succeed within the last mile network. This role will use complex problem-solving skills, manage multiple high visibility priorities, and maintain a keen attention to detail and the ability to work in a fast-paced environment with ambiguous situations.
The DSS Account Manager role will be expected to support direct inquiries from DSP program teams as well as DSPs. These support request help keep the DSP day to day business operations running and can range from invoice inquires, administrative support of systems and onboarding, as well as fleet allocation concerns. DSS was created to help DSPs and Business Coaches to scale, you must have a strong passion for accountability, setting high standards, and driving results through focus on improving existing and future state operations, systems, and processes. The ideal candidate will be able to think big to create a long-term plan and influence leaders, but have exceptional tactical skills to drive day-to-day implementation. They will be able to learn quickly and become subject-matter experts in a number of different fields.

Key job responsibilities
Providing introductory information to new DSPs, setting up accounts and providing guidance through initial onboarding and launch.
Conducting DSP training (in person and virtual) across several product types.
Support DSP intake process, solving simple and complex issues from a variety of topics.
Maintaining a positive, empathetic, and professional attitude toward customers at all times.
Communicating with customers through various channels and responding promptly to inquiries.
Maintain customer records by updating account information within Salesforce.
Developing long-term solutions using field experience and data-driven assumptions

A day in the life
As an Account Manager, DSP Shared Services, you will be responsible for specific tasks or workstreams that enable the onboarding, launch, or ongoing support of a DSP owner. You will develop mechanisms to identify problems that can be solved at scale and work cross functionally to build solutions to improve efficiency and reduce manual errors of standard work. You’ll accomplish this by analyzing problems, discovering insights, delivering recommendations, and driving change through internal stakeholder teams.

About the team
At Amazon, our goal is to provide customers with an incredible package delivery experience through the last mile of the order. To achieve this goal, we partner with a network of small independent delivery businesses (Delivery Service Providers) and the independent contractor community (Amazon Flex) to deliver customer orders. Utilizing continuous improvement initiatives and creative thinking, our Delivery Station teams ensure that millions of packages reach their final destination as efficiently as possible.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of technical recruiting in high volume and high hiring bar environment experience
- Experience building candidate sourcing and closing strategies
- Experience in technical recruiting in high volume and high hiring bar environment
- Experience recruiting for mid-senior level talent
- Experience working with essential tools of the trade, resume databases, and internet sourcing tools
jobs.description:
The Amazon Security Recruiting team is seeking a talented, customer-focused sourcing recruiter with a passion for delighting clients and candidates to join our global recruiting organization. Amazon Security's dedicated talent acquisition team plays a crucial role in identifying and attracting top security professionals across all domains. These talent acquisition specialists partner closely with security leaders to understand technical and operational requirements, driving candidate generation strategies and maintaining high hiring standards. The Amazon Security organization emphasizes a shared responsibility model, with security teams working in a distributed yet coordinated manner while maintaining centralized governance. Through continuous monitoring, automation, and regular security assessments, Amazon maintains robust protection across its diverse ecosystem. This structure, supported by strategic talent acquisition efforts, enables Amazon to scale its security operations effectively while adapting to evolving threats, ultimately protecting customers, employees, and assets through integrated security programs and technologies.

Successful sourcers on this team utilize their experience in setting benchmarks, forecasting and tracking against metrics, and prioritizing to hit customer SLAs. They thrive in an innovative, fast-paced environment, can roll up their sleeves, work hard, and have fun to get the job done. They draw on extensive knowledge of Talent Acquisition and functional areas of the business, forging dynamic relationships with business leaders and hiring managers.

Key job responsibilities
• Partner with hiring teams to build effective sourcing and assessment approaches, managing customer expectations through deep ROI understanding
• Recruit passive candidates with focus on cultural fit and motivation assessment
• Build and maintain candidate networks through proactive market research and relationship management
• Recommend and implement strategic recruitment initiatives aligned with Amazon's mission
• Develop and execute structured recruitment plans with clear deliverables and timelines
• Screen and interview candidates to create ideal candidate slates within consistent timelines
• Participate in special projects to enhance recruiting practices and identify top talent


About the team
Why Amazon Security
At Amazon, security is central to maintaining customer trust and delivering delightful customer experiences. Our organization is responsible for creating and maintaining a high bar for security across all of Amazon’s products and services. We offer talented security professionals the chance to accelerate their careers with opportunities to build experience in a wide variety of areas including cloud, devices, retail, entertainment, healthcare, operations, and physical stores.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

Inclusive Team Culture
In Amazon Security, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Ongoing DEI events and learning experiences inspire us to continue learning and to embrace our uniqueness. Addressing the toughest security challenges requires that we seek out and celebrate a diversity of ideas, perspectives, and voices.

Training and Career growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, training, and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.title: Account Manager, DSP Shared Services

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience analyzing data and best practices to assess performance drivers
- Experience with Excel
jobs.description:
The Americas Deliver Service Partner (NA DSP) team partners with entrepreneurial individuals across the globe to build sustainable and scalable delivery capacity so that Amazon can continue to delight customers with fast, efficient, and bar raising fulfillment of their orders. Equal parts advisor and operating partner, Amazon’s team of Business Coaches assist our DSP business owners as they build and grow successful last mile package delivery businesses. As trusted advisors, Business Coaches work alongside DSPs to help solve their biggest challenges at a programmatic level; influence their business planning and strategy; and look around corners to identify obstacles and recommend solutions, ensuring DSP owners make effective business decisions and are setup for success.
The DSP Shared Services team (DSS) is the backbone of the Americas DSP organization and it is their workstreams that enable the scale of the program. This Account Manager, DSP Shared Services will be responsible for providing support on a day to day processes that enable DSPs to continue to grow and succeed within the last mile network. This role will use complex problem-solving skills, manage multiple high visibility priorities, and maintain a keen attention to detail and the ability to work in a fast-paced environment with ambiguous situations.
The DSS Account Manager role will be expected to support direct inquiries from DSP program teams as well as DSPs. These support request help keep the DSP day to day business operations running and can range from invoice inquires, administrative support of systems and onboarding, as well as fleet concerns. DSS was created to help DSPs and Business Coaches to scale, you must have a strong passion for accountability, setting high standards, and driving results through focus on improving existing and future state operations, systems, and processes. The ideal candidate will be able to think big to create a long-term plan and influence leaders, but have exceptional tactical skills to drive day-to-day implementation. They will be able to learn quickly and become subject-matter experts in a number of different fields.


Key job responsibilities
- Providing introductory information to new DSPs, setting up accounts and providing guidance through initial onboarding and launch.
- Conducting DSP training (in person and virtual) across several product types.
- Support DSP intake process, solving simple and complex issues from a variety of topics.
- Maintaining a positive, empathetic, and professional attitude toward customers at all times.
- Communicating with customers through various channels and responding promptly to inquiries.
- Maintain customer records by updating account information within Salesforce.
- Developing long-term solutions using field experience and data-driven assumptions


A day in the life
As an Account Manager, DSP Shared Services, you will be responsible for specific tasks or workstreams that enable the onboarding, launch, or ongoing support of a DSP owner. You will develop mechanisms to identify problems that can be solved at scale and work cross functionally to build solutions to improve efficiency and reduce manual errors of standard work. You’ll accomplish this by analyzing problems, discovering insights, delivering recommendations, and driving change through internal stakeholder teams.

About the team
At Amazon Last Mile, our goal is to provide customers with an incredible package delivery experience through the last mile of the order. To achieve this goal, we partner with a network of small independent delivery businesses (Delivery Service Providers) and the independent contractor community (Amazon Flex) to deliver customer orders. Utilizing continuous improvement initiatives and creative thinking, our Delivery Station teams ensure that millions of packages reach their final destination as efficiently as possible.

jobs.title: Account Manager, DSP Shared Services

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of non-internship professional software development experience
- 5+ years of programming with at least one software programming language experience
- 5+ years of leading design or architecture (design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and existing systems experience
- Experience as a mentor, tech lead or leading an engineering team
- Strong background in foundational iOS/Apple platform technologies and languages such as Swift, Objective-C, and UIKit/SwiftUI
jobs.description:
Amazon Interactive Video Service (Amazon IVS) is AWS's fully managed live streaming service, built on the same battle-tested technology that powers Twitch and trusted by thousands of enterprises across gaming, social, live commerce, sports, and entertainment.

Our mission: enable developers to build communities around live video. We handle everything from ingest to processing to delivery at global scale, so developers can focus on creating interactive, engaging live experiences for their audiences. Whether you're streaming to tens of millions of concurrent viewers or just getting started, Amazon IVS removes the complexity so developers can move fast.

IVS’s Video Client SDK team is looking for experienced mobile engineers to build a multi-platform broadcasting and playback system that works everywhere people want to create and consume video. We own the Real Time, Broadcast, and Video Player SDKs that power our customers' live video experiences. As a software engineer in the Mobile Client SDK Team reporting to the Director of Client Engineering, you will create and refine a media experience used by millions of users all over the world.


Key job responsibilities
- Develop and maintain our video playback, broadcast, and real-time SDKs for iOS
- Collaborate with backend video teams that support low-latency and real-time media broadcasting and playback
- Build reliable unit, integration, and end-to-end tests
- Stay current on Apple platform developments, media frameworks, and streaming technologies

A day in the life
You might start the morning investigating a customer-reported playback issue, digging into AVFoundation behavior across iOS versions. By midmorning you're building new playback functionality in our HLS Player SDK including API design, implementing for functionality in C++, crafting platform specific bindings, and testing across a range of devices. After lunch you're reviewing a teammate's PR for the real-time SDK, discussing codec tradeoffs with the backend team, and running integration tests to make sure nothing regressed. The work spans the full media stack on Apple platforms and no two days look the same.

About the team
Amazon Interactive Video Service (IVS) is a managed live streaming solution that helps customers create interactive video experiences at scale. We power some of the world's largest streaming platforms, including Twitch, enabling businesses and developers to add live video and real-time interactivity to their applications without managing complex video infrastructure.

We build innovative solutions that democratize live video streaming technology. You'll work with talented engineers who are passionate about solving complex challenges and creating seamless, low-latency, and real-time streaming experiences. If you're excited about shaping the future of interactive live video, join us as we continue to expand our capabilities and empower customers worldwide.
jobs.description_short: Amazon Interactive Video Service (Amazon IVS) is AWS's fully managed live streaming service, built on the same battle-tested technology that powers Twitch and trusted by thousands of enterprises across

jobs.title: Account Manager - LATAM, DSP Shared Services

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in robotics, mechanical/mechatronics engineering, systems engineering or related field
- 8+ years of experience in hardware engineering or systems engineering
- Experience providing technical leadership and/or line management for cross-functional engineering teams
- Experience with hardware product development from concept through production
- Demonstrated ability to evaluate how hardware design decisions impact software and overall system performance
jobs.description:
Are you inspired by invention? Is problem solving through teamwork in your DNA? Do you like the idea of seeing how your work impacts the bigger picture? Answer yes to these questions and you'll fit right in here at Amazon Robotics.

We are building robots that pick, place, and manipulate items and packages across Amazon's fulfillment network. The Manipulation Robotics organization is looking for a Hardware Development Manager to guide our grasping and end-of-arm tooling (EOAT) team. This team designs the grasping systems for our manipulation workcells, including Robin, Sparrow, and Cardinal, as well as next-generation systems that push the boundaries of what robotic manipulation can do. You'll have the opportunity to shape the long-term technical vision for grasping and manipulation robotics, and build the roadmap for what comes next.

The team works across suction-based and finger-based grasping modalities, designing tightly coupled hardware, controls, and software systems where design decisions are deeply interconnected across the architecture. Deploying at scale means that reliability, supportability, ease of use, and cost matter just as much as raw performance. We are relentless about simplifying our designs, because simpler systems are more reliable, more cost-effective, and easier to scale.


Key job responsibilities
You will lead a team of hardware and systems engineers accountable for the design, development, and deployment of grasping systems for robotic manipulation workcells. In this role, you will:

- Drive technical direction and cross-functional alignment across hardware, software, controls, and program teams, ensuring the team is solving the right problems and moving together
- Navigate tradeoffs across performance, cost, schedule, and architecture on tightly coupled system designs, always looking for opportunities to simplify
- Champion scrappy, iterative approaches: push the team to prototype fast, test real hardware, learn fast, and iterate before committing to full designs
- Drive cost optimization and Design for X (DfX: cost, test, manufacturing, serviceability) across grasping programs, and own vendor and supplier relationships to bring better technology into our systems
- Bring together experts across disciplines, extract the key constraints and insights, and synthesize a clear forward-looking strategy for grasping and manipulation that goes beyond near-term project delivery
- Define and track metrics that measure team performance and product quality, develop engineering processes and best practices, and foster adoption of AI tools to accelerate the team
- Be a visible advocate for your team, invest in your people through growth plans and mentoring, and create an environment where technical contributors continue to develop and thrive

A day in the life
Amazon offers a full range of benefits that assist you and eligible family members, including domestic partners. Benefits can vary by location, the number of regularly scheduled hours you work, length of employment, and job status such as seasonal or temporary employment. The benefits that generally apply to regular, full-time employees include:
1. Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage
2. Maternity and Parental Leave Options
3. Paid Time Off (PTO)
4. 401(k) Plan

If you are not sure that every qualification on the list above describes you exactly, we'd still love to hear from you! At Amazon, we value people with unique backgrounds, experiences, and skillsets. If you’re passionate about this role and want to make an impact on a global scale, please apply!



About the team
This is a team of talented hardware and systems engineers who build and test real hardware every day. The team runs standups in the lab because iteration speed matters. You'll own lab space and be alongside your engineers to understand what's really happening with the hardware. The culture is hands-on, scrappy, and biased toward learning through experimentation.
